title: Summary: Turn 3
content: Pepe is a background monitoring thread that periodically checks context token utilization. When above 0.85, it runs a three-phase process: (1) LLM summarization of each conversation-turn memory, (2) local character 3-gram hashing to 256‑D vectors, and (3) cosine similarity filtering to keep only memories whose summary vector is >= 0.3 similar to the current goal. The filtered memory IDs are written back to session.selectedMemoryIds, allowing the next LLM call to use only relevant context. Pepe is optional, has a 30s cooldown, and requires at least 3 memories to trigger.
